food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — fried chicken burger, large fries, diet cola

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn class for a double crispy chicken burger combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fast food meal) used as the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKR (chicken burger, fast food style) used as the closest defensible anchor for the burger component. No composite combo-meal code exists in FoodEx2; the dominant food item (fried chicken burger) is used as the anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for a branded fast food combo meal at this specificity.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double burger + large fries + Coke Zero)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of a Double McCrispy burger (approximately 280 g), a large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and a large Coke Zero (approximately 346 g / 400 ml), totalling approximately 780 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — McCrispy, French Fries (Large), Coke Zero (Large); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — French Fries, fast food; McDonald's USA Nutrition Calculator reference values
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived by component-level aggregation: (1) Double McCrispy burger nutritional data from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition information (two McCrispy chicken fillets + bun + condiments); (2) Large Fries from McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 21137 (fast food french fries); (3) Coke Zero contributes negligible macronutrients (essentially zero calories, protein, fat, carbohydrate per 400 ml serving). Per-100g values are calculated from the combined meal weight of approximately 780 g (excluding Coke Zero liquid volume for solid-food macro density, but including Coke Zero mass for total serving weight). Because Coke Zero contributes near-zero macros, the per-100g figures reflect the burger + fries composite at ~434 g solid food mass normalised to 100 g. Energy and macros are consistent with McDonald's Singapore published nutrition panel for the McCrispy burger and large fries.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Fast food french fries GI ~63; white wheat burger bun GI ~71; composite meal GI estimated ~65.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Double McCrispy Upsized Meal as a composite product. GI is estimated from the dominant glycemic contributors: (1) white wheat burger bun GI ~71 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread analogue); (2) fast food french fries GI ~63 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Table 1); (3) fried chicken fillet contributes minimal glycemic load due to high protein and fat content. Composite meal GI estimated at 65 (medium) using weighted carbohydrate contribution method. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 78.5) / 100 ≈ 51.0, where carbohydrate per serving ≈ 78.5 g (28.6 g per 100 g × 434 g solid food / 100 × correction for Coke Zero near-zero carb contribution at ~0.4 g per 400 ml).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~430 mg per 100 g; estimated ~1,870 mg per full meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal, as a single serving may provide approximately 78–94% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2,000 mg, significantly elevating blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~51 per serving) and high refined carbohydrate content from burger bun and french fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally in reduced portions, as the high glycemic load (~51) from refined wheat and fried potato components will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; the high fat content also impairs insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol content in a single ultra-processed meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal, as it delivers substantial saturated fat (~12 g per serving), trans fat (~0.9 g per serving), cholesterol (~165 mg per serving), and sodium (~1,870 mg per serving), all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and adverse cardiovascular events.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double McCrispy Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food combo meal consisting of a Double McCrispy burger — two deep-fried crispy chicken fillets stacked in a toasted wheat bun with lettuce and mayonnaise — served alongside a large portion of salted french fries and a large Coke Zero (diet cola). The chicken fillets are coated in a seasoned crispy batter and deep-fried, producing a crunchy exterior with a juicy interior. The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. Nutritionally, this is a high-calorie, high-sodium, high-fat ultra-processed meal. A full serving (approximately 780 g including the beverage) delivers an estimated 1,175–1,200 kcal, approximately 47 g protein, 57 g fat (including ~12 g saturated fat and ~0.9 g trans fat), and ~78–80 g carbohydrate, with sodium approaching 1,870 mg — nearly the full recommended daily sodium limit. The glycemic load per serving is approximately 51, classifying it as a high glycemic load meal driven by the refined wheat bun and fried potato fries. This meal is clinically significant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease, as a single serving may substantially exceed recommended daily limits for sodium, saturated fat, and glycemic load. It is best regarded as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary choice. The meal is widely available at McDonald's outlets across Singapore and is a popular fast food option among adults and young people seeking a filling, flavourful fried chicken meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Double McCrispy Upsized Meal?
Is the Double McCrispy Meal halal in Singapore?
What is the sodium content of McDonald's Double McCrispy Upsized Meal?
Is the Double McCrispy Meal suitable for diabetics?
How much fat is in a McDonald's Double McCrispy Upsized Meal?
What is the glycemic index of a McDonald's fried chicken burger meal?
Can I eat Double McCrispy if I have high blood pressure?
What are the macros in a McDonald's Double McCrispy Upsized Meal Singapore?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived by component-level aggregation from McDonald's Singapore published nutrition information and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for fast food french fries. McDonald's Singapore publishes individual item nutrition data but not a combined meal panel; therefore, per-100g values are calculated from aggregated components and normalised to the estimated solid food mass. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed sources. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved primary source provides a direct composite meal nutrient panel for this exact product.
notes: 1. Serving weight estimated at ~780 g total (burger ~280 g + large fries ~154 g + Coke Zero ~346 g / 400 ml). Per-100g values are normalised to the solid food component mass (~434 g burger + fries) to avoid dilution by the near-zero-calorie beverage, consistent with standard food composition practice for combo meals. 2. Coke Zero contributes approximately 0.4 g carbohydrate and ~1.6 kcal per 400 ml serving; these are negligible and do not materially affect per-100g macro values. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 4. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel for these items. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 8. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 9. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 12. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 13. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 14. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 16. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 17. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 18. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 19. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 20. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 21. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 22. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the McDonald's Singapore nutrition panel. 23. GI is an analogous estimate (medium, 65) based on dominant glycemic contributors (wheat bun and french fries) from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). 24. This meal is tagged Halal per McDonald's Singapore Halal certification. 25. The ® symbol in the original product name has been retained in aliases but omitted from the canonical food_name field for indexing cleanliness.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed with component-level nutrient aggregation from McDonald's Singapore and USDA FoodData Central. All mandatory multilingual names added (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). GI estimated from analogous sources with full methodology. All -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Aliases consistency check performed — all aliases_by_language entries verified present in flat aliases list.

Iteration 2: Reviewed all hard gates and Q criteria. Confirmed G3 numeric hygiene (all -1 values documented), G4 core macro sanity (all non-zero with plausible values), G6 multilingual compliance (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi all present). Confirmed Q4 GI > 0 (65, medium). Confirmed Q8 aliases consistency. Score confirmed 8/8. Final decision: REJECT due to G5 ontology_honesty: 'approximate' is not a valid match_type for foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type.

Iteration 3: Corrected G5 ontology_honesty by changing 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' for both foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type, as per validator feedback and alignment with existing gap notes. Re-evaluated all hard gates and Q criteria. All hard gates now pass, and score remains 8/8. Final decision: PASS.
